title: Today's Markets Brief: Dow Jones Futures, Palo Alto Reverses Lower On Earnings; Google Tests Support
summary: A quick look at today's market headlines, with Dow Jones futures, Palo Alto's earnings reversal, and Google's support test.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Dow Jones Futures:, Palo Alto Reverses Lower On Earnings; Google Tests Support
imageCaption: Dow Jones Futures:, Palo Alto Reverses Lower On Earnings; Google Tests Support
imageSourceTitle: Dow Jones Futures:, Palo Alto Reverses Lower On Earnings; Google Tests Support

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your markets brief. I am looking at some interesting movements in the markets today, and I'm excited to walk you through them.
Dow Jones futures are in focus today, along with Palo Alto's earnings report and Google's stock action. Let's dive into each of these stories.

Dow Jones futures are seeing some movement, but what's really catching my attention is Palo Alto's reversal lower on earnings. The company reported its quarterly results, and it seems investors were not impressed. My read is that the earnings expectations were likely too high, and the stock got a bit ahead of itself. Now, it's reversing lower, which is a pretty sharp correction.

Also, oil prices are climbing today after reports of U.S.-Iran trade tensions. It seems there's been a bit of a back-and-forth between the two countries, with Trump saying negotiations are still underway. This is all impacting oil prices, which are jumping around a bit. I mean, who doesn't love a good game of geopolitical ping-pong, right?

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
